overview.title: The Waterworks Campaign
overview.BriefSummary: The expertly skilled C0010 campaign targeted water and wastewater systems using spear-phishing techniques, exploiting vulnerabilities, and deploying disruptive malware.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2017-9805 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office memory corruption vulnerability
stage.description: The C0010 campaign crafted convincing emails targeting specific employees in water and wastewater systems. These emails contained malicious attachments exploiting a Microsoft Office memory corruption vulnerability (CVE-2017-9805). Upon opening the attachment, the malicious payload was executed, providing the attacker with initial access to the victim's system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-20481 [CWE-487]: Unrestricted File Upload Vulnerability in Windows WMI Provider Host
stage.description: After gaining initial access, the attacker exploited an unrestricted file upload vulnerability in the Windows WMI Provider Host (CVE-2024-20481) to remotely execute commands on compromised systems. This allowed them to establish an initial foothold within the targeted network.

Scenario.summary: The C0010 campaign demonstrated advanced skills by employing spear-phishing techniques, exploiting vulnerabilities, and deploying disruptive malware to target critical water and wastewater systems. Their ultimate goal appears to be financial gain through potential ransom demands or data extortion.
